  • Bridging "Nature" and "Spirit": The CRMhs Ontology for the Integration of Heritage Science and Cultural Heritage Data [#131, Moderately Innovative]
  • Bridging the Semantic Gap: A Review of Data Interoperability Challenges and Advanced Methodologies from BIM to LCA [#147, Incremental]
  • Ontology-driven energy management in smart buildings: A comprehensive review of methodologies, tools, and challenges [#164, Incremental]
  • Leveraging Ontology-based Systems through Continuous Ontology Engineering [#171, Incremental]
  • Adapted Ontology Development Process: A Co-Simulation Scenario Ontology for Energy Systems [#181, Routine]

Reactive & Combinatorial Problems — avg: 38 (Incremental)

These papers tackle fundamental problems in combinatorics, stochastic processes, and discrete optimization. Reaction-Diffusion on Fully-Connected Lattice studies A+A→A coalescence processes, an infinite-dimensional system exhibiting mean-field behavior. Co-design Threading for Quantum Circuits partitions algorithms across multiple QPUs to overcome qubit limitations. Engineering Local Search for Maximum Independent Set on Hypergraphs addresses a nearly unstudied but practically relevant combinatorial problem in high-order correlations. From Data to Laws discovers conservation laws without false positives under parameter variation. Graph Pattern Mining for Anomalous Business Partnerships identifies non-obvious high-value collaborations. Together these represent frontier challenges in mathematical foundations underlying complex systems.
  • Reaction-diffusion on the fully-connected lattice: A + A → A [#116, Moderately Innovative]
  • Engineering Local Search for the Maximum Independent Set Problem on Hypergraphs [#158, Incremental]
  • Graph Pattern Mining for Anomalous Business Partnerships [#168, Incremental]

Domain-Specific & Applied AI — avg: 38 (Incremental)

These papers apply AI to specialized, often niche domains. AgriTrust (Federated Semantic Governance) enables trusted agricultural data sharing through semantic governance frameworks meeting regulatory requirements like EUDR. Challenges for AI in Fusion Energy surveys AI applications to materials, plasma control, and simulation across a computationally intensive, data-scarce domain. Folklorism in Poetry analyzes whether folkloric elements function as structural frameworks or superficial borrowing in Uzbek poetry. Intelligent System for Personnel Risk Assessment applies ML to insider threat detection in critical infrastructure. Sperm Whale Birth documents a rare cetacean birth event using behavior analysis. The Ontological Status of AI-Generated Music explores philosophical implications of indistinguishable AI and human composition. These papers show AI enables solutions in previously inaccessible domains when paired with domain expertise.
  • Description of a collaborative sperm whale birth and shifts in coda vocal styles during key events [#40, Innovative]
  • The Ontological Status of AI-Generated Music: From Stylistic Mimicry to Collaborative Agency [#118, Moderately Innovative]
  • Challenges and opportunities for AI to help deliver fusion energy [#156, Incremental]
  • The AgriTrust Framework: Federated Semantic Governance for Trusted and Interoperable Agricultural Data Sharing [#165, Incremental]
  • A Comprehensive Approach to Building an Intelligent System for Proactive Personnel Risk Assessment in Critical Infrastructure [#174, Incremental]
  • Ontological Dimensions of Folklorism in the Poetry of Matnazar Abdulhakim [#180, Routine]

Domain-Specific Knowledge Systems — avg: 26 (Incremental)

These papers apply semantic frameworks to specialized knowledge domains. BIM Semantic Mapping enables cross-model data integration for AEC (architecture/engineering/construction) by resolving naming conflicts and structural hierarchies. Semantic Web Knowledge Graphs for Healthcare organizes EHRs, clinical notes, lab results, imaging, and genomics into coherent patient-centered representations. Modelling Food and Mood with Ontology-Driven RAG connects nutrition and mental health recommendations through structured knowledge graphs. Ontology-driven Personalized IR for XML demonstrates that understanding user knowledge and goals enables personalized retrieval beyond one-size-fits-all results. Together these show domain-specific ontologies unlock interoperability and personalization where generic approaches fail.
  • Modelling Food and Mood Relation with Dynamic Personas: An Ontology-Driven RAG-Based Recommendation Approach [#151, Incremental]
  • Semantic Web Knowledge Graphs for Patient-Centred Healthcare: A Systematic Review and Design Framework [#166, Incremental]
  • Semantic Mapping and Cross-Model Data Integration in BIM: A Lightweight and Scalable Schedule-Level Workflow [#176, Incremental]
  • Ontology-driven personalized information retrieval for XML documents [#177, Routine]
